hypothesis: After commitment-level causal-use evidence passes, a reversible memory lifecycle stops stale memory influence under a deterministic world shift and restores useful memory after recurrence.

controls.description: Suppress an unrelated active memory and require the shifted commitment to remain unchanged.
Keep the stale target family active after the v3 shift.
Keep the preserved memory unavailable when v2 recurs.
Suppress the still-valid target family under v2 and require no quarantine decision.

gates.criterion: Target-family suppression repairs commitment while target-only and placebo suppression do not.
Quarantine and retirement stop stale target and descendant influence after the v3 shift.
A bounded v2 recurrence probe reactivates the preserved memory family and restores success.
Every family status change has a contiguous typed evidence receipt and all four persistent states are observed.
